article_title: Pregnanolone Glutamate, a Novel Use-Dependent NMDA Receptor Inhibitor, Exerts Antidepressant-Like Properties in Animal Models

aggregated_relations.subject: PG
aggregated_relations.object: time spent in the open arms
evidence_sentences: One-way ANOVAs revealed that PG administered at the dose of 1 mg/kg significantly increased the time spent in the open arms, F(2, 24) = 7.654, p = 0.0027 and decreased the time spent in the central platform, F(2, 24) = 10.01, p = 0.0007.

aggregated_relations.subject: PG
evidence_sentences: Chronic administration of PG at the dose of 1 mg/kg over 30 days reduced aggressive actions in singly housed mice exhibiting aggressive behavior on the first interaction.
Chronic administration of PG at the dose of 1 mg/kg over 30 days reduced aggressive actions in singly housed mice exhibiting aggressive behavior on the first interaction.
The duration was significantly reduced after 14 and 30 days of PG 1 mg/kg administration, compared to the scores of vehicle treated controls in the first day.

aggregated_relations.subject: Memantine
relation_raw_variants: results in the preferential blockade of
has been shown to result in the preferential blockade of
evidence_sentences: Memantine has been shown to result in the preferential blockade of excessive NMDAR activity, while sparing normal excitatory synaptic function (Lipton, 2006, 2007).

relation_raw_variants: has a more pronounced inhibitory action on

aggregated_relations.subject: PG
evidence_sentences: PG administration led to the significant decrease of aggressive behavior in the agonistic interactions with non-aggressive group-housed partners, when compared to the control interaction in the first day of the experiment.

aggregated_relations.subject: Stress induced by social isolation
aggregated_relations.object: a significant decrease in pregnenolone, progesterone, allotetrahydrodeoxycorticosterone, and allopregnanolone concentrations
evidence_sentences: Stress induced by social isolation causes a significant decrease in pregnenolone, progesterone, allotetrahydrodeoxycorticosterone, and allopregnanolone concentrations in the cerebral cortex, compared to the group-housed controls (Serra et al., 2000).

aggregated_relations.subject: Chronic stress
aggregated_relations.object: the production of allopregnanolone, as well as other GABAergic neurosteroids
evidence_sentences: Chronic stress reduces the production of allopregnanolone, as well as other GABAergic neurosteroids (Serra et al., 2000).

aggregated_relations.subject: A reduced ACTH response to chronic stress
aggregated_relations.object: hyper-responsiveness of the hypothalamic–pituitary–adrenal axis (HPA) axis to the new stimuli
evidence_sentences: A reduced ACTH response to chronic stress leads to hyper-responsiveness of the hypothalamic–pituitary–adrenal axis (HPA) axis to the new stimuli, and confers vulnerability to mood and anxiety-related disorders, as well as depression (Hoffman et al., 1985).

aggregated_relations.subject: A reduced ACTH response to chronic stress
aggregated_relations.object: vulnerability to mood and anxiety-related disorders, as well as depression
evidence_sentences: A reduced ACTH response to chronic stress leads to hyper-responsiveness of the hypothalamic–pituitary–adrenal axis (HPA) axis to the new stimuli, and confers vulnerability to mood and anxiety-related disorders, as well as depression (Hoffman et al., 1985).

aggregated_relations.subject: Antidepressant treatment
evidence_sentences: Antidepressant treatment normalizes the altered allopregnanolone levels (Uzunov et al., 1996; Uzunova et al., 1998; Schüle et al., 2014) suggesting that GABAergic neurotransmission alteration by the neurosteroids has a therapeutic effect.

aggregated_relations.subject: DHEA and DHEAS
evidence_sentences: Therefore, it might be surprising that DHEA and DHEAS as positive NMDA and negative GABAA modulators exert antidepressant activity (Wolkowitz et al., 1997, 1999; Urani et al., 2001).

aggregated_relations.subject: PG
evidence_sentences: Similarly, PG displays more potent inhibition of responses mediated by NR1/NR2C-D receptors, compared to those mediated by NR1/NR2A-B receptors (Petrovic et al., 2005).

aggregated_relations.subject: Stress
evidence_sentences: Stress induced by social isolation causes a significant decrease in pregnenolone, progesterone, allotetrahydrodeoxycorticosterone, and allopregnanolone concentrations in the cerebral cortex, compared to the group-housed controls (Serra et al., 2000).

causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: Chronic stress reduces the production of allopregnanolone, as well as other GABAergic neurosteroids (Serra et al., 2000).

causal_tuples.subject: reduced ACTH response to chronic stress
causal_tuples.object: hyper-responsiveness of the HPA axis to new stimuli
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: A reduced ACTH response to chronic stress leads to hyper-responsiveness of the hypothalamic–pituitary–adrenal axis (HPA) axis to the new stimuli, and confers vulnerability to mood and anxiety-related disorders, as well as depression (Biggio and Purdy, 2001).

causal_tuples.subject: Antidepressant treatment
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: Antidepressant treatment normalizes the altered allopregnanolone levels (Uzunov et al., 1996; Uzunova et al., 1998; Schüle et al., 2014) suggesting that GABAergic neurotransmission alteration by the neurosteroids has a therapeutic effect.

causal_tuples.subject: DHEA
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: Therefore, it might be surprising that DHEA and DHEAS as positive NMDA and negative GABAA modulators exert antidepressant activity (Wolkowitz et al., 1997, 1999; Urani et al., 2001).

causal_tuples.subject: DHEAS
